summary: Iteration 4 — full backend (25 pytest tests) + frontend E2E testing. Backend: Packages CRUD + Mongo seed + WeasyPrint PDF + Newsletter (subscribe/list/export/delete) + Stats. Found and FIXED 1 backend bug: GET /api/packages/haji-khusus/pdf returned 500 because Content-Disposition header contained em-dash (U+2014) from fullTitle which is not latin-1 encodable. Sanitized filename to ASCII in /app/backend/server.py — all 4 packages now serve PDFs. Frontend: homepage packages-section renders 4 cards via API, newsletter-section form submits and shows success state, /packages/umrah-economy renders gallery-carousel (5 imgs, prev/next, thumbs), both print-itinerary-btn and detail-pdf-btn link to ${BACKEND}/api/packages/{id}/pdf. Admin: 4 sidebar nav items, admin-packages-page list, admin-pkg-editor loads existing package with pkg-published-switch, /admin/newsletter shows export-csv-btn. Old /print/packages/:id route REMOVED (react-router warns 'No routes matched'). 25/25 backend tests pass after fix; ~95%+ of frontend assertions pass.

minor.issue: FIXED — non-ASCII chars (em-dash) in fullTitle broke Content-Disposition. Sanitized to ASCII.

issues: When already authenticated and going directly to /admin/packages in a fresh tab without prior session, page redirects to /admin/login (expected behavior, just noting). Token appears to be persisted in localStorage and works on same-tab navigation.

action_items: (Optional) Add data-testid='admin-package-row-{id}' or 'admin-pkg-new-btn' to admin packages list rows / new button for easier future automated testing — currently we relied on href-based selectors which work but are less explicit.
(Optional) export-csv-btn renders as a <button> instead of an <a href>; it triggers download via fetch+blob (works) but href attribute is null — fine functionally.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py has grown to ~568 lines covering auth/inquiries/blog/packages/PDF/newsletter — still under 700-line guideline but consider splitting into routers (auth.py, packages.py, newsletter.py, blog.py) before next iteration to keep DRY.
PDF filename sanitisation should be applied for ANY Content-Disposition header (e.g., CSV export uses static name so OK). Suggest a tiny helper `_safe_filename(name)` in pdf_render or utils.
Newsletter POST returns 200 for already-subscribed instead of 200/409 — spec explicitly mandates idempotent 200 with alreadySubscribed=true which is correctly implemented. Good.
All endpoints use Pydantic models with EmailStr; 422 validation working as expected.
Public package GET correctly filters published=true; admin GET returns all incl drafts. Good separation.
MongoDB _id correctly excluded everywhere via projection {'_id': 0}.
WeasyPrint generates ~36KB+ PDFs for full packages and ~5–30KB for sparser ones — quality OK.
Frontend GalleryCarousel correctly handles prev/next/thumbnails/lightbox (5 images including hero on umrah-economy).
seed_data_creation: Backend test created one temporary package (TEST_pkg_*) which was deleted in same test, and one newsletter subscriber (test_*@example.com) which was also deleted. No residual TEST_* data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Public URL: https://umrah-landing.preview.emergentagent.com. Backend test suite at /app/backend/tests/test_iter4.py (25 tests, all pass). Admin login admin@babatour.com / BabaTour2026!. Seeded packages: umrah-economy, umrah-vip, umrah-plus, haji-khusus. Bug fixed this iter: PDF filename header now ASCII-only. Old /print/packages/:id route fully removed — confirmed by react-router warning. Use data-testid='gallery-carousel', 'print-itinerary-btn', 'detail-pdf-btn', 'newsletter-section', 'packages-section', 'admin-packages-page', 'admin-pkg-editor', 'pkg-published-switch', 'export-csv-btn', 'admin-nav-{inquiries|packages|blog|newsletter}'. Carryover (still NOT implemented from previous iters): httpOnly cookies, brute-force lockout — JWT in localStorage is current auth model.
rca of the issue: haji-khusus.fullTitle contained an em-dash '—' (U+2014). server.py built Content-Disposition: inline; filename="Itinerary_<name>.pdf". Starlette encodes HTTP headers as latin-1 which cannot represent U+2014, raising UnicodeEncodeError → 500. Fix: encode filename to ASCII with errors='ignore' before embedding. Reproduction: curl -i $URL/api/packages/haji-khusus/pdf → 500 before fix, 200 application/pdf after fix. Mitigation: filename sanitisation applied at /app/backend/server.py:454-457.
